svg.text: 9.14 · Crash recovery — roll forward committed, roll back in-flight
on restart: nothing committed is lost, nothing uncommitted survives, replicas agree (the module's payoff)
scan for torn pages (bad checksum) → restore intact copies from the doublewrite area → recovery operates on VALID pages
1 · REDO APPLY (roll FORWARD) — from the checkpoint LSN (9.6)
replay redo: re-apply each committed change IF the page's LSN shows it wasn't applied (idempotent, 9.6)
→ every COMMITTED transfer durable in redo but not yet flushed is re-applied → NOTHING committed lost (M07/7.5)
transactions IN-FLIGHT (uncommitted) at crash → reverse their partial changes (atomicity, M07/7.2)
→ the half-done transfer is fully UNDONE → no half-applied money
prepared-in-redo but uncertain? IN the binlog → commit (roll forward); NOT in binlog → roll back
→ redo log & binlog end up agreeing on the SAME committed set → replicas / PITR consistent (M10)
exactly the committed transfers, none of the in-flight ones — "the server died mid-transfer, and it's all correct"
this IS the ARIES algorithm: redo-then-undo with LSNs · the durable log is the source of truth, recovery replays it
recovery TIME ∝ redo to replay (checkpoint age, 9.6) · when recovery CAN'T recover (corruption, lying disk) → M15 (innodb_force_recovery)
